sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. Watson is an 86-year-old male who returns for follow-up for adult hydrocephalus.
He has a 10-year history of progressive gait impairment, cognitive impairment, and decreased bladder control.
Since shunt surgery on August 1, 2020, he reports improved walking ability, able to walk to the dining room without assistance, but still requires help at times.
He experiences urinary incontinence and wears a diaper, but does not have sores or painful urination.
He also reports improved cognitive function, able to have normal conversations without feeling lost.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Significant for impaired ability to carry out daily functions, urinary incontinence, continued walking impairment, and cognitive impairment.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 124/80, heart rate 64, respirations 18, weight 174 pounds, and pain 0 out of 10.
Physical exam: The shunt site was clean, dry, and intact with a confirmed setting of 2.
The patient was able to converse easily, and the cranial nerves, pupils, eoms, face, and motor function were normal.
The gait score was 6-8 out of 12, and the balance score was 12 out of 16 for a total score of 18-20 out of 28.
Test Results: CT scan from 10/15/2020 shows a frontal hornstan at the level of firm and of menroma of 4.6 cm with a third ventricular contour that is flat with a span of 10 mm.
There is a tiny amount of blood in the right frontal region with a tiny subdermal collection, and substantial small vessel ischemic change.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mr. Watson has made significant improvements since shunt surgery, but still experiences gait impairment, urinary incontinence, and cognitive impairment.
Problem list: Adult hydrocephalus; Gait impairment; Urinary incontinence; Cognitive impairment.
Plan:
I will continue to monitor Mr. Watson's condition and adjust the shunt as needed.
I will see him again in 3 months to reassess his condition and discuss the possibility of driving again.
I will also recommend that he enroll in a driver's safety program to assess his ability to drive safely.
Follow-up: I will see Mr. Watson in 3 months to reassess his condition and discuss the possibility of driving again.
